data.title: Siemens shares down 9% as management sees sluggish private capex, says 'not participating' in a crucial segment
data.description:
In the call with analysts, Siemens confirmed that the 'private capex has not picked up meaningfully'.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Siemens were sharply lower by nearly 9% in mid-day trade, after analysts were disappointed by the fine print of the investor call that the company hosted on December 20, which alluded to a stagnant private capex, and concerns regarding supply chain for digital industries.

In the call with analysts, the company confirmed that the 'private capex has not picked up meaningfully', souring the sentiment for the share price. However, private capex in semiconductors, batteries, and the electric vehicles space has been good, the company added.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company said that it is seeing traditional technologies garner slower investment, and added that Siemens is 'not participating in LCCs' in India or globally. A Line-Commutated Converter (LCC) is a device to convert alternating current (AC) into direct current (DC) and vice versa, and are commonly used in High-Voltage DC (HVDC) systems.

The company also mentioned that it remains active in the Voltage Source Converter (VSC) segment, but the numbers here are 'not material' right now. VSCs are devices to connect high voltage alternating current (HVAC) and high voltage direct current (HVDC) systems.

Siemens shares fell to near a month low, however, on a YTD basis are higher by 75%. The company has a market capitalisation of Rs 2.55 lakh crore.

The sentimental ruboff was seen in the shares of ABB India as well, which slipped by nearly 4% during trade.

This is being updated.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: DAM Capital IPO gets subscribed 5x on Day 2 so far; retail, NIIs lead; GMP surges
data.description:
DAM Capital Advisors IPO continues robust subscription on day 2 of the issue.

DAM Capital Advisors IPO continues to garner robust public subscription on day 2 of the share sale, led by retail and non-institutional investors segment.

The initial share sale of investment bank DAM Capital Advisors Ltd got fully subscribed hours after opening for bidding on Thursday and ended with 2.75 times subscription.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The initial public offering (IPO) received bids for 10.04 crore shares against 2.08 crore shares on offer, reflecting 4.83 times public subscription, as per NSE data at 1:10 PM.

Retail individual investors (RIIs) part attracted 6.62 times subscription while the quota for non-institutional investors got subscribed 6.98 times. The category for qualified institutional buyers (QIBs) received 2 percent subscription.

Check All IPO News Here

Earlier, the issue raised Rs 251 crore from anchor investors.

data.title: Trump threatens tariffs If EU doesn’t buy more US oil and gas
data.description:
US president-elect Donald Trump in Paris, France, on Saturday, Dec. 7, 2024.

President-elect Donald Trump has threatened the European Union with tariffs if its member countries don’t buy more American oil and gas.

“I told the European Union that they must make up their tremendous deficit with the United States by the large scale purchase of our oil and gas. Otherwise, it is TARIFFS all the way!!!,” he said on Truth Social.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The US is the world’s largest producer of crude oil and the biggest exporter of liquefied natural gas. LNG buyers — including the EU and Vietnam — have already talked about purchasing more fuel from the US, in part to deter the threat of tariffs.

US president-elect Donald Trump, at the Elysee Palace, in Paris, France, on Saturday, Dec. 7, 2024. There's still more than a month until inauguration day, but Donald Trump isn't waiting: he makes his first trip abroad since November's election, heading to Paris for the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral. EU officials and member states have been bracing for a trade offensive ever since Trump’s election victory last month.

The bloc was largely caught off-guard in 2017 when Trump, citing national security concerns in his previous term as president, levied tariffs on European steel and aluminium. Since then, the EU has reinvented its trade doctrine and expanded its toolbox, giving it a range of options to counter coercive practices.

“We are well-prepared for the possibility that things will become different with a new US administration,” German Foreign Minister Annalena Baerbock said after a Group of Seven meeting in Italy in late November. “If the new US administration pursues an ‘America first’ policy in the sectors of climate or trade, then our response will be ‘Europe united.’”

The EU’s new anti-coercion instrument strengthens trade defenses and enables the commission to impose tariffs or other punitive measures in response to such politically motivated restrictions.

The EU also adopted a so-called foreign subsidies regulation, which allows the commission to prevent foreign companies that receive unfair state handouts from participating in public tenders or merger-and-acquisition deals in the bloc, among other measures.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Trump has multiple grievances against the EU and has criticized Europe for not spending enough on defense and for the US-EU trade deficit. He once referred to Brussels, the seat of the EU institutions, as a hellhole, and more recently he said he’d once told a NATO member that he’d let Russia do “whatever the hell they want” to it if it didn’t hit defense spending targets.

Trump has threatened tariffs against countries from China to Canada, and is particularly focused on nations that have trade deficits with the US. Europe is already the top destination for American LNG, with more than half of the deliveries going to the continent last year.

US has also become a major exporter of crude oil over the past decade or so, sending so-called light as well as medium density varieties to Canada and also countries across Europe and Asia.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Sensex falls 1,000 pts from day's high amid post-Fed jitters; banks, IT top drags
data.description:
Tech Mahindra, Axis Bank, UltraTech Cement,TCS, and L&T were the major laggards.

In a very volatile session, benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex traded in the negative on December 20, extending their decline to five days after IT and realty stocks dented sentiment in the market. The sharp fall in technology stocks comes despite Accenture reporting better-than-expected first-quarter earnings in the US. On the other hand, metal and oil and gas stocks bucked the trend.

At noon, the Sensex was down 420 points or 0.5 percent at 78,792 while the Nifty traded lower by 112 points or 0.5 percent at 23,839. On the BSE, 1,661 stocks advanced, 2,180 stocks declined and 138 shares remain unchanged.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

"The FII buying witnessed in early December is getting reversed now and that's getting reflected in market trends with large caps, particularly financials, coming under pressure. However, this trend is unlikely to be sustained and, therefore, retail investors can adopt a buy-on-dips strategy as quality large caps will soon bounce back," V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Financial Services, said. He added that the negative response to the Fed’s commentary on interest rate cuts in 2025 will be temporary.

The Nifty IT index slipped 2, paring all losses as profit booking set in after an initial gain of nearly 1 percent. A decline in major names such as TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and Coforge dragged the index lower. Optimism over Accenture’s revised outlook had also lifted Infosys and Wipro’s American Depository Receipts (ADRs) on the NYSE, which jumped 2-3 percent overnight. Nifty Bank, PSU Bank index, and Realty index also tanked almost a percent each. Bank major names such as HDFC Bank, SBI, and ICICI plunged to drag the index lower.

Also read: India's richest investors: Ashish Kacholia, Mukul Agrawal's wealth soars in 2024, Hemendra Kothari, RK Damani's portfolio saw erosion

Nifty Metal gained 0.7 percent, supported by expectations of easing energy costs. Experts believe that if the ongoing conflict subsides or stabilizes, it could further bolster the sector’s outlook. Nifty Oil and Gas index also rose on the back of sharp gains in GAIL, ONGC, and Indian Oil Corporation. Energy stocks also rose led by Adani Power and NTPC.

The Nifty mid-small cap indices mirrored weak trends with losses of 0.7 and 0.4 percent, respectively. According to Ruchit Jain, Vice President of Technical Research at Motilal Oswal, the activity in this segment is largely stock-specific, with third-quarter earnings expected to shape the trajectory in the months ahead.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

As for individual stocks, the General Insurance Corporation of India stock was on a roll on December 20, leaping nearly 11 percent to hit a record high of Rs 494.45, driven by a gush of trading volumes. The stock witnessed an upsurge in trading volumes in the counter as around one crore shares changed hands on the exchanges so far, already well ahead of the one-month daily traded average of 14 lakh shares.

Read more: Nacdac Infra's Rs 10-crore IPO receives bids worth Rs 14,000 crore: 10 things to know about Ghaziabad-based SME

FirstCry parents Brainbees Solutions also gained 3 percen after domestic brokerage JM Financial kicked off coverage on the stock with a bullish 'buy' tag, seeing  a target price of Rs 692.The price target implies an upside potential of 14 percent from the previous session's closing price. FirstCry India's multi-channel accounts for 20 percent+ market share of organised (online + brick and mortar) childcare market in India with online market share of roughly 24 percent.

Shares of MTAR Technologies surged nearly 10 percent on December 20, buoyed by the company's latest Rs 226 crore order wins. The company secured these orders for its clean energy and aerospace segments. Out of the total, orders worth Rs 191 crore have been secured for various products from Bloom Energy in the clean energy sector.

The Nifty extended its losses for a fourth straight session yesterday but found support near 23,870, the level seen during the November 28 derivatives expiry. This area also aligns with the 61.8 percent retracement of the November-December rally and the rising 200-day moving average. The three-day RSI, a momentum indicator, dropped below 10, a level that has historically signaled either a pause in the downtrend or a short-term recovery, says Akshay Chinchalkar, Head of Research at Axis Securities.

Dr Reddy's, Hindalco, Maruti Suzuki, Adani Enterprises, and Apollo Hospitals were the top gainers on the Nifty. Tech Mahindra, Axis Bank, UltraTech Cement,TCS, and L&T were the major laggards.

data.title: Gravita India's board approves Rs 1,000 crore QIP; stock pares early gains
data.description:
Over the past year, the stock has recorded a 12-month run of 130 percent.

Gravita India Ltd. stock price rose in the early session, but pared its gains after the firm's board of directors approved a Qualified Institutions Placement (QIP) to raise up to Rs 1,000 crore.

Gravita India's board approved the issue price for 47,70,537 equity shares at Rs 2,096.20 per share, including a premium of Rs 2,094.20. This price reflects a Rs 110.29 discount (5 percent) on the floor price of Rs 2,206.49, as permitted under SEBI regulations.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 12.45 pm, Gravita India shares were trading at Rs 2,393.8, down 0.4 percent on the NSE.

Oxbow Master Fund and Mirae Asset Mutual Fund picked up 5.9 lakh shares or 12.4 percent of the issue, respectively. Goldman Sachs picked up a five percent piece of the issue for its India Equity Portfolio. Aditya Birla Sun Life was also allotted five percent of the equity shares of the issue.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

This fund raise will likely be used for to repay outstanding borrowings, meet working capital requirements, additional capex, M&A opportunities, debt reduction and other general corporate purposes. "We are looking at some opportunities in merger and acquisitions across globe. So we want ourselves to be ready as and when such an opportunity is there," CEO Yogesh Malhotra had told analysts in October.

The company is on track to set up a pilot project of lithium-ion battery recycling, and its first rubber recycling plant in Mundra. Gravita India said it is making steady progress towards increasing its capacity to over 5 lakh metric tonne per annum by FY27. The company has investment plans of Rs 600 crore which includes capex for existing as well as new verticals, like lithium-ion, rubber and steel recycling.

Over the past year, the stock has recorded a 12-month run of 130 percent, in comparison to the Nifty 50's gain of 15 percent during the same time.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

data.title: Dr Reddy's Labs top Nifty gainer, surges 9% in three sessions
data.description:
Trading volumes for Dr Reddy's were already double the one-month average.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Dr Reddy's Labs emerged as the top gainer on the Nifty 50 with its over 3 percent surge on December 20. With gains in today's session, the stock also extended its uptrend to the third consecutive session, clocking in a 9 percent upmove during the period.

At 12.22 pm, shares of Dr Reddy's were trading at Rs 1,356.66 on the NSE. The stock also witnessed an uptick in trading volumes in the counter which strengthened its upmove. Around 46 lakh shares changed hands on the exchanges so far, already double the one-month daily traded average of 23 lakh shares.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Shares of the drugmaker were also on the list of major gainers in the previous session, even when the overall market bore the brunt of heavy selling. Analysts attributed the recent buying into pharma stocks, including Dr Reddy's to a strategic shift in positions towards defensive plays amid times of uncertainty in the market.

Follow our market blog for live updates

In addition to that, sentiment for Dr Reddy's also improved after brokerage firm Nomura upgraded its rating on the drugmaker to a 'buy' as against the earlier 'neutral' call with a price target of Rs 1,500. According to the brokerage, the stock's relative underperformance indicated that concerns regarding the high contribution of Revlimid were already priced in.

Nomura also noted that the drugmaker's capital expenditure has more than doubled in FY25, which according to the firm, reflects Dr Reddy's commitment to enhancing its operational capabilities. Additionally, Nomura notes that potential upside from GLP-1 and biosimilars is not fully factored into the FY27 estimates, offering further growth potential for the drugmaker.

In the first half of FY25, Dr Reddy's allocated significant investments into improving its manufacturing infrastructure and ramping-up its biosimilars, peptides, and other therapies to aid future growth.

data.title: Nacdac Infra's Rs 10-crore IPO receives bids worth Rs 14,000 crore: 10 things to know about Ghaziabad-based SME
data.description:
Nacdac Infrastructure IPO GMP today price is indicating a full 90 percent listing gains on December 24.

Nacdac Infrastructure IPO allotment is due today following a record subscription of nearly 2,000 times to Rs 10.01 crore SME issue which has grabbed many eyeballs.

Ghaziabad-based company approached the primary market to raise Rs 10.01 crore through IPO of 28.6 lakh shares at the upper price band.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Here are key things to know about Nacdac Infrastructure IPO

1) Nacdac Infra concluded the maiden share sale with a bumper public subscription of 1,976.14 times, according to the data available with the exchange.

2) Nacdac Infra IPO which opened on December 17, and concluded on December 19 had a price band of Rs 33 - 35 per share with a lot size of 4,000 shares.

3) The Rs 7.28-crore issue attracted over Rs 14,000 crore in bids from Rs 6,41,048 number of applications. About Rs 2,73,00,000 were allocated to the anchors.

4) The portion for non-institutional investors attracted 2,635.49x subscription while the share of Retail Individual Investors (RIIs) got subscribed 2,503.667 times. The QIB portion was subscribed 236.39 times.

5) Investors subscribed for a whopping 411.03 crore equity shares as against the offer size of 20.80 lakh shares.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Check All IPO News Here

6) Nacdac Infra IPO shares will be listed on the BSE on December 24, while the allotment of shares is expected to be announced today.

7) Nacdac Infra IPO objective: The firm will utilise issue proceeds for its working capital, and general corporate purposes.

8) Nacdac Infra IPO GMP Today Price: According to market observers tracking the grey market activities, the shares of Nacdac Infra may list at a premium of 90 percent. Investorgain quoted a GMP of Rs 50 over the IPO price in the unofficial market, indicating a bumper listing.

9) Nacdac Infrastructure is a Ghaziabad-based construction company. The diversified construction services offered by Nacdac Infrastructure Limited includes end-to-end solutions for a broad spectrum of construction and infrastructure development projects.

10)This was the highest ever subscription numbers by IPO in the SME segment. Hamps Bio, Toss The Coin, HOAC Foods India, Kay Cee Energy & Infra were the other IPOs recording more than 1,000 times subscription.

In the ongoing financial year up to October 15, 2024, a total of 159 small and medium enterprises (SMEs) have collectively raised over Rs 5,700 crore through initial public offerings (IPOs). Notably, FY24 saw a record year for SME public issues, with 196 companies successfully mobilising more than Rs 6,000 crore from the market.

data.title: General Insurance Corp spurts 11%, hits record high amid heavy volumes
data.description:
Shares of GIC surged close to 32 percent during the past month.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of General Insurance Corporation of India were on a roll on December 20, leaping nearly 11 percent to hit a record high of Rs 494.45, driven by a gush of trading volumes.

The stock witnessed an upsurge in trading volumes in the counter as around one crore shares changed hands on the exchanges so far, already well ahead of the one-month daily traded average of 14 lakh shares.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 11.48 am, shares of GIC were trading at Rs 488.80 on the NSE. The stock has been buzzing in the past month as it surged close to 32 percent during this period.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

For the September quarter, the domestic reinsurer company reported a 15.9 percent on-year increase in net profit, coming at Rs 1,860.76 crore in Q2 FY25, up from Rs 1,605.09 crore in the same period last year. The growth was driven by strong investment income and a reduction in commission costs. On a sequential basis, the net profit surged by 74.4 percent from Rs 1,036.06 crore in the previous quarter.

At the end of the September quarter, GIC's solvency ratio stood at 342 percent, a sharp spike from 282 percent from the year ago period. The company’s combined ratio narrowed to 114.05 percent from 115.83 percent during the same period. The reinsurer also reported a 26 percent reduction in its underwriting loss, which fell to Rs 1,088.43 crore from Rs 1,471.61 crore.

data.title: Brainbees Solutions gains 3% as JM Financial initiates coverage with 'buy' rating
data.description:
Over the past year, shares of Brainbees Solutions have fallen around 8 percent.

FirstCry operator Brainbees Solutions Ltd. gained four percent in the morning session as domestic brokerage JM Financial kicked off coverage on the stock with a bullish 'buy' tag, seeing  a target price of Rs 692.

The price target implies an upside potential of 14 percent from the previous session's closing price.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 11.45 am, shares of Brainbees Solutions were trading at Rs 624.9 per share, up 3.1 percent on the NSE.

Follow our live blog to catch all the updates

FirstCry India's multi-channel accounts for 20 percent+ market share of organised (online + brick and mortar) childcare market in India with online market share of roughly 24 percent. "While the consumers still have potential options when their children cross 4-5 years of age, FirstCry has an extremely dominant market positioning for 0-4 year age group," noted JM Financial.

FirstCry has honed a successful playbook over 15 years. In 2019, it expanded to the UAE, followed by Saudi Arabia in 2022, tapping into a $10 billion opportunity by FY29. While UAE operations will stay online, KSA will replicate India's multi-channel model, with combined profitability expected by FY27.

The company targets gross margin expansion of about 6.5 percent by FY28, driven by higher contribution from FirstCry home brands, COCO store expansion reducing franchisee margin sharing, economies of scale boosting manufacturing margins, and improved take-rate negotiations with third-party brand partners.

Key risks to JM Financial's assessment include: COCO store expansion cannibalising existing outlets, execution hurdles in the international segment, and increased competition from QC players or Meesho eroding market share.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Over the past year, shares of Brainbees Solutions have fallen around 8 percent. During the same time, the Nifty 50 index has gained around 13 percent in trade.

data.title: Investors only have themselves to blame as Jay Powell steals Christmas
data.description:
Powell has done us all a favour in reminding us that next year will not be for the faint of heart.

Katie Martin 

For markets, US Federal Reserve chief Jay Powell is the Grinch who stole Christmas. But the festive shakeout in bonds, currencies and stocks now under way in the wake of the US central bank’s latest pronouncements is a mini-crisis of investors’ own making.

Fed meetings, and the minutiae of its public statements, are always marquee events for investors, setting the tone across all major asset classes. Wednesday’s meeting, the last of 2024, always came with the potential
